preamble: The voiced palatal approximant /j/ can undergo a historical phonetic transformation to the voiceless alveolar plosive /t/. This shift often occurs as part of a larger sound change chain, involving fortition, devoicing, and a shift in the place of articulation.
phoneticEffects: Fortition (approximant to plosive), devoicing (voiced to voiceless), and a shift in place of articulation from palatal to alveolar

examples.note: As part of the first palatalization of velars in Balto-Slavonic, the voiced velar plosive /g/ shifted to /j/, which subsequently transformed into /t/.

sources: Velcheva, B. (1989). Proto-Slavic and Old Bulgarian Sound Changes. Slavica.
